reviews.text:
awful experience; had to constantly chase after them for follow-ups and feedback; ended up firing her and going with another law firm that was excellent. tiffani and her staff basically were advocating on behalf of my opponent (my HOA) and NOT on my behalf.

apparently she's changing her name now so be aware (soon to be prestige law firm, pllc).

reviews.ownerResponse: After reviewing our records, we are unable to identify an attorney-client relationship between Noah Wolff and Tiffani N. Brown Law, PLLC with respect to the matter described. We strive to ensure that reviews accurately reflect experiences with Tiffani N. Brown Law, PLLC and respectfully encourage you to review.

reviews.text: I now have rendered Tiffani N. Brown Law’s services for two separate small claims matters over the past year. Both matters resulted in a favorable outcome for us. We maintained adequate communication throughout the process both via email with Tiffani and via text with her paralegal Rebecca. I would recommend them for assistance on any small claims matter.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for taking the time to share your experience. We truly appreciated the opportunity to assist you with both matters and are glad to hear that you were satisfied with the outcomes and our communication throughout the process. Your recommendation means a great deal to our team. Please do not hesitate to reach out if we can be of assistance again in the future.

reviews.text: The service was quick and Tiffani responded to all my emails promptly.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your kind feedback! We are glad to hear that you were satisfied with the responsiveness and service. We truly appreciate you taking the time to share your experience and are here to assist.

reviews.text: Called the office about a liability insurance concern and the person I spoke with asked for the Metlife Legal Plan eligibility ID which I provided.  She said an attorney will research and call back.  After not hearing back for over a week, called them back and they again said an attorney would call back but never heard anything.

Unfortunately my experience with Tiffani has not been the best. I utilized her services for small claims assistance through my MetLife Legal Plan.

It was a relatively straightforward case but after *months* I received documentation with another person’s name on it. I felt like that was a sign of lack of detail which I would not really want in an attorney. I also felt like it was a privacy concern sending someone else’s documents. It is a good thing this was not something more serious.

In those months I had to constantly email and call for follow ups. I have not even discussed the case with the attorney herself because she is unreachable. I have been extremely patient and hate to leave a bad review, but the last straw was the fact that I had called multiple times and they told me message would be sent to the attorney but it has been weeks with nothing. It is frustrating to have to keep following up as if this was my kid and I was trying to get them to do their homework.

To give an idea I reached out to her in the beginning of March and still didn’t get complete paperwork until now (beginning of Aug).

My experience with Tiffani has been wonderful. She was patient in understanding my concern (even when I was rambling and giving her lots of information). Through my chaos, she provided an organized, succinct approach to how we would address the issue. She was able to take the mounds of documents and information I provided, digest, and regurgitate it back it a document that was provided to the defendant. It was PERFECT!
She was very professional and communicative along the way. I felt heard and seen throughout the process. Hopefully I don't need to use her again, but if I need an attorney, I will definitely be calling Tiffany N. Brown Law!
Tiffany Brown is reliable professional and very intelligent she works hard and gets the job done

Tiffani is an intelligent, strong minded, and kind woman. She has the knowledge and qualities I needed in an attorney. I am thankful I reached out to her and she had my matter taken care of in a professional matter. Happy with my outcome and the quality of service that I received.

Tiffani is very patient,  knowledgeable, and affordable! I was in need of an attorney for a personal family matter, and frustrated with the entire situation, Tiffani took on my case and made the process very smooth! I was satisfied with the service and end result! Thanks Tiff!
Tiffani is professional, knowledgeable, and RESPONSIVE!!! Feeling taken care of is huge for me and Tiffani did just that!
Attorney Brown is an extremely dedicated & caring attorney that makes sure her clients are always satisfied. She cares about her clients like her own family
Attorney Brown has worked diligently to help me and my family prepare for the future. She cares about my future, my family’s future and advocates for what is best for us.

Tiffani is one the most caring people I have been blessed to know. I reached out to her over the Summer 2020 and I was so sure that thanks to COVID and a very cantankerous (soon to be) ex-husband that my process would be forever long and drawn out. Tiffani made sure that wasn't the case. She held my hand every step of the way. Answered every question and concern I had. There were times that I was worried that I wasn't doing enough to protect my children and Tiffani helped me to see what I was doing and how I had everything set up was in my children's best interest. She kept me calm despite the storm. A mother herself, she will never stop fighting for you!
reviews.ownerResponse: Mary, thank you very much for the review and kind words. I know how crazy COVID has made everything and I'm happy that I could be there for you when you needed it!

reviews.text: Tiffany is always available to help when I have legal questions. She is caring and always willing to help!! I whole heartedly recommend Tiffany Brown!
reviews.ownerResponse: Julia, thank you for the kind words and the review! I'm glad I was able to assist you.

reviews.text: Friendly staff.
Everything you expect and more.

My interaction with Jim was truly impactful. I had a situation I needed consultation on and reached out to various law firms, but Jim was the only one who was not only responsive, but genuinely helpful, sharing resources and guidance even before I had officially booked time with him.
After sharing my story, I was so relieved by his honesty and understanding. He was there every step of the way, bringing his extensive knowledge and experience to bear on my case. He was able to negotiate my separation, and I received three times the amount I expected , with no litigation.
If you are looking for someone who truly hears you and moves with intention, Jim is absolutely the one.

I cannot say enough good things about Jim Garrity. During a very stressful and uncertain time, he provided not only expert legal guidance but also reassurance and clarity. He was patient, direct, and extremely responsive whenever I had questions.

He genuinely cares about his clients and it shows in how he communicates and prepares. He went above and beyond to help  I’m so grateful to have had him in my corner. I highly recommend him to anyone needing strong, trustworthy representation.

Jim was a pleasure to deal with, and even though getting the settlement had multiple delays and bumps in the road, the issues were taken care of in a timely fashion. Great communication the whole way through, couldn't have asked for a better lawyer. Thanks Jim!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks, Dan, from our entire firm!  You have been an amazing client.  Good luck and let us know if we can help in the future!

Jim deserves a 10 star review. He is absolutely the best lawyer in town. He is professional, timely and knowledgeable in his specialty. He was readily available when I needed his assistance or had questions about my case. I strongly recommend him, and very happy with my case outcome.

I had the privilege of working with Jim Garrity during one of the most stressful times of my life, and I could not be more grateful for the representation I received. From the very first consultation with Jim, it was clear that I was working with someone who possesses both incredibly deep legal expertise and a genuine dedication to protecting their client’s best interests.  He is an amazing  expert on employee rights - he was able to answer every question I ever asked without hesitation, and in great detail - and every step of the process was carefully thought through and explained to me in a way that gave me complete confidence. What stood out most was his ability to fight aggressively on my behalf while remaining compassionate and supportive toward me. He is relentless, sharp, and strategic, which I loved. He left no detail overlooked. But with me he was approachable, patient, and truly kind. And he treated me like his partner in the case, always making sure my concerns were heard and my questions answered. My case reached a far better outcome than I thought possible. I wholeheartedly recommend Jim Garrity to anyone who wants an aggressive powerhouse expert in employee rights combined with real human care. Ten stars!

Karlee

Jim did a wonderful job and made sure justice was served!!!! No matter what the situation is he will get the job done with his awesome team…. He is the one I will always go to he is the best of the best!!! 😁

Jim has truly helped change my life. His knowledge and expertise is pristine. He is reliable, timely and responsive. He is thorough, kind and truly one of the best men and lawyers I have ever met. Highly recommend.

Experience cant be replaced.  Jim Garrity is great.  His experience with employment law is second to none.  Can't give him enough praise.

I cannot say enough about my recent experience as a client of Mr. Jim  Garrity. He made me feel at ease from the moment that we met with his kind and confident demeanor. This attorney not only knows the law inside and out, he is an expert in the field of employee rights and discrimination. He is trustworthy and approachable. He won’t sugarcoat anything for you, but instead will tell you the truth about how he feels regarding your case, explaining his thoughts and opinions in great detail, piece by piece, from A to Z. He is thorough and precise. He leaves no stone unturned. He made me feel as though I was his only client, never too busy to take my calls, always responsive via text or email in a timely manner. Bottom line, you couldn’t ask for a better attorney to have in your corner during this very unique and specialized field of practice. Jim cares a great deal about the outcome of your case and will work tirelessly to get you the justice you deserve.
His staff is equally impressive and dedicated to that positive outcome for you as well. I can’t say enough about his paralegal Wanda, she is as genuine as they come, with a warm and inviting smile, full of southern charm and determination. Just like Jim, she is there for you from day one, truly making you feel as though no other client exists, you are the one and only one that matters. She will answer your questions and guide you along the way, always there for you, supporting you in every way that she can. I don’t think in over a year there was ever a time that she didn’t answer her phone when I called.
On a personal note, feeling as though you were discriminated against by your employer, feeling as though your rights as an employee and human being were violated are feelings that one should never have to endure, specifically in the workplace. I almost walked away from what happened to me, wanting to just put it behind me and move on. I am so glad that I didn’t. I am so glad that instead, I picked up the phone and called Jim.  One of the best decisions that I have ever made.
He will tell you and so will I, you are worth the fight. Let your voice be heard and let your story be told, for you will pave the way and set the precedent for those that come after you. Let Jim be your hero so that you can be theirs….

Called there office the female that answered the phone had mouth full of Food!!! I started talking an she very rudely cut me off and told me " what I had to tell her didn't matter that I needed to fill out a intake form" I started trying to speak again and before I could get a single word out she said " It doesn't matter Intake form" I then told her that Google reviews matter and she said " not really" and hung up.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Daren.  I'm terribly sorry to hear about your experience.  Unfortunately, you didn't call me directly at Jim Garrity - Employee Rights Lawyer (850-345-1098).  You called a different number (850-383-4800) for a different law firm in the same building.  I can be reached at that second number, but it's that firm who employs the receptionist you talked to, not me.  I didn't hire her, don't employ her, don't supervise her, and have no control over her.  I do wish you'd reached out to me before posting a review that harms my name and reputation, so that I could point you to the right law firm and business owner - the one that you actually called.  Again, it wasn't me or anyone who works for me.  If you would like to talk, I'm all for it.  You can reach me at Jim@JimGarrityLaw.com.  And I do hope you'll consider removing this review, because you wound up hitting the wrong person.  That would mean a lot to me.  Thanks again.  Jim

reviews.text: I call Jim Garrity every time I have an employment problem, and so does the rest of my family.  We have been using him for more than 20 years. Sometimes you just get wrongly fired, or you can't get promoted or hired.  He's always available, always easy to talk to, and always has some incredibly smart ideas and strategies. He's helped my adult children avoid terminations, get paid commissions they were being cheated of, and get accommodations for disabilities on the job. He helped me get workers' compensation benefits (which my boss was trying to deny) and helped us in so many other situations.  He's also helped my neighbors and several friends in job-related problems.  I wouldn't even consider using anyone else, and I certainly wouldn't recommend anyone else.
James was an amazing resource and very kindly guided me through some perilous waters. I highly recommend reaching out to him for any of your employment law concerns.

Jim was extremely helpful, responsive and on-point with a recent consult.  Honesty and clear assessment of my situation.  He came highly recommended from a friend/prior client and now I know why.  Thank you, Jim! I would recommend that you reach out to Jim Garrity.
He never got back to me I'm still waiting

reviews.text: Thanks Becky for all your help and hard work. You really made a big difference in my life by helping me settle my car accident case. I appreciate you so much. Thanks!

reviews.text: I was in an ATV accident and almost died. I literally was in a coma for over a week. All because a jeep that was off roading in the area I was riding was drunk and pretty much ran me over. I was determined to get justice and was sure as heak not gunna be stuck with hundreds of thousands of dollars in medical bills from my hospital stay and 7 surgeries over an 11 month span, along with over two years of physical therapy. I hired Matt Dolman who was known to be aggressive when it came to representing his clients in serious traumatic matters like mine. He went back in forth with the insurance company for well over a year but eventually ended up getting me almost a one million dollar settlement. I seriously don't know where I would be today if it wasn't for Matt. On another note, I am continuing to recover and improve from the accident and while I will still need more time in rehab for my injuries, the doctor said I was ahead of schedule with my progress. So I am happy to say the least.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hey John. I remember how tough your case was. I am so glad that things have gotten better for you. Thank you for coming on here and taking the time to leave us a review. It really means a lot to me. I hope your recovery continues to go well.

reviews.text: A car hit me when it pulled out in front of me after failing to yield. The driver was going very fast and could have nearly killed me. I still ended up needing surgery on my neck and back and would have to go through months of rehab. I contacted Dolman Law because I have heard nothing but great things and they really worked around the clock for me to ensure the best outcome possible. They are very professional, sound minded, communicate well and put their client's interests above all. I highly suggest working with them. They literally saved my life from being a living hell because had it not been for them, I dont' know how on earth I would have ever been able to afford paying all those medical bills.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for this lovely review, Crystal! We are proud to represent our injured car accident clients after someone else's reckless behavior has caused them harm. I'm glad that we were able to negotiate a settlement that acknowledged the true value of your damages, including your medical bills for surgery and rehab. If you ever need us, we are here for you. Our team wishes you a full and speedy recovery!

He settled my case and kept all the money he's no longer a lawyer he has been permanently disbarred.
Worst experience ever 4 years never seen a dime

DO NOT HIRE! DO NOT HIRE! 0 stars, 2 thumbs down, and 2 middle fingers! Do not hire Mr. Gus Soto for anything. You will regret it. While he rarely (and I mean almost never) will respond to your emails, phone calls, and text messages, his office manager, Gigi Mcmullen, aka Mrs.Gus Soto, aka his wife, will almost always respond but not however with the information you have repeatedly requested. There is always some excuse (lie) about why the settlement disbursement hasn’t been made yet, 1) Didn’t like the MSA wording, 2) Georgia Bar has to approve it, 3) There are liens (there were none) 4) Have to wait for the check to clear (over 2 years later), 5) The computer got COVID, just to name a few. On the rare occasions he bothers to respond, he forgot which lie he told you yesterday, so it’s a new one today. Don’t believe a word that comes out of his mouth and don’t trust any papers he gives you.  If you have ever hired/used this attorney, get a copy of your official settlement document from the court in which it was filed and compare it to what you were told or given by him or his staff. It may have been changed, to his benefit. Don’t be surprised if you find out you didn’t get all the money the judge who signed off on it believes you did.

I've read many of the reviews of our law firm.  Most are glowing.  I thank those clients for their kind words.  The reviews that bother me are the clients who did not get at they wanted or needed from our law firm.  I noted a couple of reviews by people we never actually represented.  I noted some where the client claimed we were unresponsive.  One client accused me of not caring while admitting she never took my calls or answered my letters.  Google makes you star your review.  I'd like to think we deserve 5 stars but I don't want to come off as arrogant.  What I do know is our firm fights for every client and we do care.  You don't do this work for 38 years unless you care about people and want to help them.
They are very helpful. Very professional. Explain everything and gave ne updates on my case.

A team that will advocate for you and your family to the highest extent! If you have a work comp situation or even an automobile accident, for all your injury needs call Anderson & Hart for representation! You won’t regret it!

I am not one to write a bad review on anyone, however Paul Anderson is the most unprofessional person I have ever dealt with.  After a serious head injury at work in March of 2024, I was referred to his office.  He seemed very professional in the beginning but was very hard to reach.  In June I had an urgent concern and left a message for him to contact me.  To this day, he has not responded.  I started receiving questionnaires wanting updates from his office.  I never acknowledged them because it was simply a waste of my time.  Then Vivian, from his office contacted me a couple of times to see how I was doing and if I wanted to proceed with the case.  I have permanent disability’s so I agreed to try again.  Vivian was awesome and very supportive.  She set up a conference call between Paul Anderson and I.  The scheduled date came and went with no response and no contact.  I waited a week and called and left a message with Vivian to find out what was going on.  Again, no response.  I have been advised by family and friends to contact a reliable and honest attorney.  Unfortunately, this has all been too much and I am just trying to make the best of my disabilities caused by my injury.  Think twice about trying to work with Paul Anderson because it’s obvious he doesn’t care.
They are really great .. Nice,patient, dedicated and hardworking..

I would never let this law firm represent me ever again. Mr. Anderson and his crew lies about everything. There were a lot hidden information, never sent proof to prove my case, called my witness day before medication for deposition, never returned phone calls, emails, and missed meeting that was scheduled. Mr. Anderson hung up the phone on me and stop answer my emails when trying to get assistance. Think really hard about this law firm before using them. They will avoid your phone calls the elderly lady that sits at the from desk oh and will not pass the message alone.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Anonymous Reviewer,

I have recently got injured on job, and was told at the ER that I might need surgery and needed further medical treatments. And needed a workers comp case number to see a doctor and start treatment. It was a nightmare dealing with my employer to provide me with a case number, and she kept refusing and telling me to just tell the doctors you’re a cash patient instead. That’s when I figured I needed legal representation, and after lots of searches and calls in the area, I felt comfortable with Anderson and Hart, Stephanie was there answered the phone call and spoke to Karla Hart and took my case. From that time she was always on point and got me the claim number quickly, and got all medical treatments needed. Stephanie Karla’s Assistant is the best, she’ll always call and email with updates regarding the claim, she’ll answer all questions and Karla will always answer her phone and text messages quickly. They handled my case very good and got all medical treatment appointments fast. They’re always there for you and care about their clients, and they’re always one call away. Big thanks to Karla and Stephanie

They protected and defended my Worker Compensation rights professionally, respectfully, intelligently, and diligently.

The entire staff of Anderson & Hart was more than willing to listen to my concerns, they protected me, they defended me, and they counseled me at the most frustrating moment associated with the insurance carrier.

Regarding my attorney, Mr. Paul Anderson. I cannot find the appropriate words to describe how thankful I am for his professional and honest guidance. Mr. Anderson, you're amazing, and I will always keep you in my prayers and in a very special place inside my heart. Last but not least, Mss. Charlotte Endefield was as professional and diligent as Mr. Anderson.

I don't know how many people will read this review, but I just want to let each of you know that the process is not an easy one, but if the law firm of Anderson & Hart takes your case, then you will be in the best hands you could be.

My God bless each of them as well as their entire family for years to come!

Highly Recommend Attorney Mike Bauer – A True Advocate in Family Law

I came to Attorney Mike Bauer with a family law case that had been completely mishandled by a previous attorney (Derrick McBurrows). I was heartbroken and felt scammed during one of the most vulnerable times of my life. While driving one day, I happened to see Attorney Mike’s office and decided to stop in. I met his wife, Kristy Bauer, who also serves as his assistant. She warmly welcomed me and scheduled a consultation — and from that point on, everything changed.

Attorney Mike and Kristy worked tirelessly on my case for two years. They were consistent, honest, and never once increased their fee. They kept me informed every step of the way and fought for the best possible outcome.

Because of their dedication, I walked away with shared 50/50 custody of my 8-year-old son, no child support, and was relieved of a $16,000 debt that was allegedly owed to my son’s mother. Attorney Mike knows his way around the courtroom and has strong relationships with other attorneys, which made negotiations smoother and more effective.

I truly cannot thank them enough. If you're looking for someone who will fight for you and stand by you, Attorney Mike Bauer is the one to call.

I used Michael back in 2018 in a horrific custody case, with an ex-husband who was classified as a sexual predator. Michael did everything in his power to make sure that we kept those no contact orders in place, and demanded that my ex have to see a psychosexual evaluator, which had findings that were actually nightmarish, But which resulted in lifetime no contact orders between him and our 2 children. I wanted to come and leave this review even though it's so many years later, because he actually was just locked back up for doing the exact same thing to multiple children who he was allowed around in his life. Mr Bauer tried to do everything in his power to make sure that this man actually got locked up back in 2018 for being around kids again, we took the probation officer All of the proof that he needed to violate, He did nothing, So now my ex has been allowed to hurt more little girls.. hopefully this will end in a prison sentence for him this time.. But thank you Mr Bauer and Kristy for protecting my two children from him the way that you did.

Mr Mike is a liar and a thief. He took $1,500 dollars from me for a case, then completely ignored my calls, this is after he made $5,000 dollars off me and we lost the case.

I wouldn't be here today if it weren't for Mr Bauer and his amazing team to help me overcome the worst accusation imaginable. I can't thank him enough. From me and my family, your the best. I would definitely recommend and insist on his expertise if needing attorney services.

On December 1, 2023, after two days of trial, my husband walked out of that courthouse a free man and was able to go home to his family. We owe that to Mr. Bauer.

I will always refer anyone in need to Mr. Bauer. He is very well educated, communicative and keeps every case so close to his heart. He fights for his clients and does everything it takes for justice to be served.

reviews.ownerResponse: I do not believe that I would tell someone to pray for another lawyer to help him or her. I have always given free consults when my time permits, but time is all that I have to sell. So we have provided free consultations,  but it costs money to operate a business and we have charged a sliding scale for consultations  for the last several years. Pro Bono cases (free of charge ) cannot be taken every day or I would go out of business. Some cases take a lot more time than others. So i have to decide which I can afford to undertake. I also have take fees to keep the doors open, so no given caller can demand my time when it is not available. When a person comes to my office and is willing to value my time, I  always take into consideration their resources and reducing the consult from $250.00 to $100.00 $50.00 or $0.00 depending ont he circumstances. So since I am constantly asking questions at no charge, I do not feel that I owe every caller my  undivided time.

I reached out to Tor Friedman in a moment of genuine crisis. The police had followed up on an old warrant, and I had no idea what to do or what might happen next. From our very first conversation, Tor gave me the reassurance I needed. His calm, confident presence grounded me when I was completely overwhelmed.

Tor handled everything with incredible efficiency. He filed the paperwork right away, kept me informed throughout the process, and was quick to answer my numerous questions. I was particularly worried about the expense, but I didn't need to be. He offered a rate that was more than fair and gave me a generous window for payment.

More importantly, he made me feel like he genuinely cared about my case. He was empathetic and non-judgmental, qualities that especially stood out when dealing with such a stressful (and frankly embarrassing) situation.

Thanks to Tor, that situation is resolved now. The charge was dismissed and I'm free to move on with my life. I'd recommend his services to anyone who needs legal help, especially those who are feeling lost / don't know where to start. He guided me through a truly frightening experience with patience, clarity, and compassion, making it clear every step of the way that he was 100% in my corner.
Tor has been a pleasure to work with. He handled everything fast and efficient and has kept me at peace of mind. He has your best interest at heart and is happy to help!

I highly recommend Friedman & Abrahamsen if you’re dealing with legal issues and want a team that actually shows up for you.

reviews.text: My wife is a state employee as am I. My wife has experienced, Discrimination, Harassment and Retaliation at work, when reporting the discrimination to leadership. False statements were put in her personnel file after reporting the discrimination. We contacted Cruz Law Firm for guidance. they charged us a $250.00 Consultation fee to tell my wife to look for another job. And said she needs to report the situation to HR. She reported to leadership above HR and experienced retaliation. Our complaint is why did Cruz Law Firm charge us $250.00 to tell her find another job?? We are writing this negative review because they should have just told her find another job and not charged us anything. We sent all the documents they requested they knew they didn't want to assist us. It is always the same thing with attorneys, they make the money and the one asking for guidance pays money and receives only frustration. We learned our lesson and will not contact Cruz Law Firm and tell anyone that asks us the same thing that they can not be trusted. you just have to pay!!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We're sorry to hear about your frustration with your experience.

Our consultation fee ensures dedicated attorney time for document review, legal analysis, and candid legal advice about the strength of potential claims and available options. The consultation fee included reviewing all of the documents you provided and the nearly hour long videoconference consultation your wife had with one of our attorneys. While we understand this may not always be the outcome someone hopes for, part of our ethical obligation is to provide honest assessments—even when that means advising that pursuing a particular legal action may not be currently viable.
These more in depth consultations allow us to thoroughly evaluate the facts and law to determine whether representation would be appropriate and provide advice to protect any potential claims. A consultation fee does not guarantee that we will take a case, but rather ensures that potential clients receive our full professional attention and guidance during that evaluation.

We take all feedback seriously and strive to ensure our consultation process and fees are clearly communicated from the outset. We regret that you felt your concerns were not addressed to your satisfaction.

I’m incredibly grateful for Tiffany’s exceptional representation over the past two and a half years. After I filed an EEOC complaint in 2021, I was wrongfully terminated from my position as a deputy. That experience turned my life upside down—but from day one, Tiffany believed in me and committed herself to fighting for justice on my behalf.

Her dedication, strategic thinking, and unwavering support were key in helping me navigate one of the most difficult chapters of my life. Tiffany made sure I was informed, empowered, and prepared every step of the way.

Her performance at trial was nothing shy of greatness. She broke down complex facts, exposed contradictions in the defendant’s case, and delivered a closing argument that left a lasting impression on everyone in the courtroom. Resulting in a jury verdict in less than 90mins!

I highly recommend Tiffany to anyone in need of a compassionate attorney. Her expertise, integrity, and commitment to justice truly make a difference.

Thank you, to the entire Cruz Law team, for standing up for the underdog and helping to right the wrongs. Your work is bigger than one case—it gives people like me hope.

reviews.ownerResponse:
We regret you feel your experience did not match your expectations. We are known for getting top-notch results for our clients while being exceptionally personable. We consistently strive for excellence and what you have described is certainly not in line with our brand.
What you have described indicates the firm did not successfully resolve your claim (which in fact, the firm did successfully resolve the claim); or that the attorney did not work diligently to resolve your concerns (you verbally admitted that the attorney always worked hard to assist you and was very prompt when communicating with you). Considering the foregoing and very respectfully, we take exception with what you have stated.

Since the posting of your allegations, the attorney has personally reached out to you to discuss and address your concerns and pursuant to that conversation, we trust that your concerns have now been resolved. Just as was communicated to you verbally, we are honored that you reached out to us, and we are pleased that our office was able to reach the most favorable result for you! We sincerely wish you the very best.

For prospective clients reading this review, please be advised that as a firm with integrity, and in the interests of fairness, it is important to point out inaccuracies, contradictions, and misrepresentations. For more information on how we can assist you, we encourage you to call the firm.

reviews.text: I contacted Amber Hall regarding a personal injury case that had already been filed. She said she would need to consult an expert before agreeing to take the case and would get back to me the following week. The next week she said two experts she consulted were testifying and it would be another week. Ten days later she had a family emergency and would get back to me the following week. When she called that following Sunday I expressed my concern that we were going to miss the deadline to respond, she instructed me to request a 30 day extension- which I did. She called the following week and said two experts confirmed there is strong evidence the defendants are liable for the damage; however, in the middle of the conversation her alarm went off for a zoom meeting and she would get back to me. After nearly ANOTHER week went by and she failed to get in touch with me as promised I sent several emails requesting an answer- to which she AGAIN did not respond. Six weeks after initially contacting Amber Hall she finally said she would not take the case. Stringing someone along for six weeks is not only unprofessional, it’s down right inconsiderate. I read several positive reviews about this attorney, but if this is indicative of how she handles cases, I’m glad she is not representing us.
reviews.ownerResponse: We’re sorry you feel that way. While the firm cannot discuss the details of the claim, the firm went to great lengths to gather the necessary information to try and pursue a case that did not have the benefit of having a lawyer on it from the very beginning.  Many times, during our review and investigation of a potential case, we identify one or more factors that raise concerns and ultimately prevent us from pursuing it and this was the case here.  Despite our good faith efforts to find more information about the specifics of the case, we ultimately decided we could not assist. Unfortunately, we cannot take every case and despite the firm being unable to assist with this matter, our hope remains the same—that your loved one receives the justice that he is seeking. We sincerely wish you all the best.

reviews.text: My experience with James Kole spanned from Sept. 2018 through the end of Jan. 2020. I wanted a local firm that specifically worked with SSA cases and went with Proctor & Kole as I was impressed with their initial responsiveness. I regret, however, not following my gut instinct from my initial meeting with Mr. Kole and sought legal assistance elsewhere. As others have stated, he is arrogant, callous and wants to be the only one talking. My case was handled very poorly from the first meeting to when I finally shut it down. I closed out my case for the single reason that because my attorney and his staff did not work with me, at all, he was wasting my time while my life was going down the drain. He thought my case was a slam dunk and, technically, I should have had a solid case; but, you NEVER leave even a sliver of opportunity for the judge, or anyone involved in the process, to find anything to deny your case.  Mr. Kole should know this since he's been practicing for 26 years, which he will tell you over and over. I have a solid decade of working in compliance and enforcement in environmental law against a vast array of industries across the State of FL, so I am experienced in building and pursing legal cases. My disability case was ridiculously simple in comparison.  Because Mr. Kole did not work with me AND did not let me see my case file prior to my hearing, we went to the hearing with a case file that was missing well over 100 pages of records, including a statement from my treating physician (which the state had requested); and, there were a number of incorrect statements on my medical records.  The judge pounced on every single conflicting/ incorrect record and used them to deny my case. The judge asked Mr. Kole at the hearing if "his client" had reviewed the case file to which he replied, "Yes", which was a lie. I asked him prior to the hearing if I had the tough judge (which I suspected I did based on my own research).  He said, "No", until after the hearing when he said that I did. If you know you are going before the toughest judge, wouldn't you do everything to ensure your clients case file is complete, correct and air tight?  How can you defend your client if you don't work with your client?  When I asked him why he told the judge I had seen my file when I hadn't, he replied that it was his job to take care of that.  How would he know what is correct, incorrect or missing from my file??  This is the very definition of gross negligence.  If I had known he was not going to go over my case file with me prior to the hearing, I would have gotten my file from the SSA and I would not have gone to my hearing until my records were complete and correct. I am frankly stunned at how badly this was handled.  I trusted Mr. Kole. That will never happen, again.
reviews.ownerResponse: The Rules Regulating the Florida Bar prevent us from discussing any details of our representation of this or any other client.  We can say, however, that our offices treat every case very seriously, understanding that our every action and words may determine the outcome of the case.  Our livelihoods rely on this work ethic as we take every case on a contingency basis and receive no fees or costs unless we win.  Frankly, we’re competitive at everything we do, even outside of work.  We can’t win every case, but we try to do so and hate it when we don’t.

reviews.text: I was arrested on false charges and was again wrongly violated for the pretrial I had been placed on and had a warrant taken out for my arrest. I then found out because of the bogus arrest I was then yet AGAIN wrongly violated in another county for a case that was supposed to already be closed. I felt totally helpless. Desperately grasping at straws I emailed legal aid and explained my situation. First thing the next morning Ryan called me. My email had gotten forwarded to him and before he even called me had already pulled up both cases and read what was going on.  He asked me what was going on and patiently listened while I explained. He agreed the cases were bogus and knew I probably couldn't afford an attorney but he knew without one I didn't stand a chance. He worked with me and came up with a price I could handle and jumped right on both cases. In 1 week he had gotten both violations withdrawn and both cases dismissed. He kept in close contact with me and kept me updated every step of the way. I don't know what compelled him to read my email that morning and he could've just deleted it and moved on but he didn't and he changed my life. I had Tim Jansen once as an attorney and he is probably the best criminal defense attorney in Tallahassee but now if anyone asks me I'll tell them that Ryan Mologhan is the next big thing and you would be crazy to look anywhere beyond him. He is the best attorney I have ever met and blows all the others out of the water!  Thank you so much Ryan! Thank you for believing in me and saving me from this corrupt system! You did an amazing job and I will recommend you to anyone who is looking for a caring, compassionate and hard working criminal defense attorney.  If I had the option I would give you a 10 star rating because 5 just doesnt seem like enough.

Juliette is a longtime friend of mine, and the level of care and guidance she consistently provides is truly exceptional. Recently, my mother faced a difficult housing situation, she could no longer afford her rent and had to move out. Neither of us had experience navigating the legal or logistical side of tenant rights, and it was a highly stressful time.

Thankfully, I reached out to Juliette. Despite her busy schedule, she made time to speak with both my mother and me on a call. She walked us through the legal process clearly. This included what steps must happen before an eviction, how to respond appropriately, and how to ensure everything was handled in writing to create a proper record.

Because of Juliette’s knowledge and her advice to communicate formally and request an extension in writing, my mother was granted the time she needed to move out peacefully and without confrontation! Her guidance not only helped us avoid unnecessary conflict but also gave my mother peace of mind in a very uncertain time.

Beyond her expertise, Juliette brings a calm, compassionate, and supportive presence. She’s not only professional and well-informed but she’s also deeply caring. You don’t just receive her skills, you receive her heart.

I highly recommend Juliette for any support or consultation in areas related to housing, advocacy, or general guidance. She is someone you want in your corner, you are guaranteed to be caring hands!

reviews.text: Very nice people

Allen was great through most of my process. Bankruptcy went very smooth. After that I needed information from the same case. Didn't realize I could look it up myself. He got very frustrated and asked me for 50 dollars basically because I didn't say thank you to him. I was dealing with the IRS and very confused at the time, didn't realize my case was public information. Which he said after I sent him the courtesy money. Keep track of your information and don't ask for help afterwards. Some people don't like being nice and doing someone that doesn't know things a favor.
reviews.ownerResponse: Ah, right.  I sometimes forget that after being paid once I am supposed to work for free for the rest of my life for a client.  As Abraham Lincoln noted, "A lawyer's time and advice are his stock in trade."  Would anyone go to a plumber, an electrician, or the grocer, and say, "I paid you a couple of years ago to do something for me, now I need something else.  And I expect you to do it for free." ?

reviews.text: Allen Turnage is a knowledgeable bankruptcy attorney that I would recommend to anyone seeking bankruptcy help. Allen is very concerned and will help you in anyway he can. I would give him a 10 if it was available.

Your recommendation means a lot to us, and we're grateful for the opportunity to have helped you through this process. If you ever need assistance in the future, please don't hesitate to reach out. Thank you again for sharing your feedback!

reviews.text:
Mr. Turnage was very helpful in saving my home. The bank agreed but refused to work inside the bankruptcy so I basically filed bankruptcy for nothing.  I was advised to convert from a Chapter 13 to Chapter 7 after that and I didn't understand why. Mr. Turnage knew I was a graduate student and should have known this would exclude me from future student loans. I did not know this because student loans are excluded from bankruptcy protection so in my mind, it doesn't matter if I have a 7 or a 13.  I saved my house but now I have to fund the last year of graduate school out of pocket, which will mean a delay for me. It's frustrating because, as my attorney, he should have known this and advised differently. I depended on his expertise and, in this case, it did not serve me well.

I understand Mr. Turnage position, and I don't want to convey the idea that he is not an effective bankruptcy attorney. If you have other life situations outside of finances (like graduate school), be cognizant of your options so that you make the best decisions FOR YOU. In hindsight, I would not convert to a Chapter 7. It was pointless in my case. Mr. Turnage is a a knowledgeable bankruptcy attorney but advocate for yourself so that other parts of your life will not be further disrupted.
reviews.ownerResponse: Your bankruptcy was not filed for nothing. We converted to Chapter 7 when the bank refused to negotiate because you needed to surrender your car, which was under water by $5000 or more, and discharge $15,000 in credit card debt. If your case had been dismissed you would still owe that money. If you were still in chapter 13 you would still be paying on the debt. So the thousands of dollars that you saved by converting your car to chapter 7 could have gone to pay this tuition and you would not have to borrow money now.

reviews.ownerResponse:
The above reviewer contacted the law office of Attorney Paul Arnold numerous times requesting pro bono services, but our office declined to extend free services and communicated this to the caller. This caller was referred and took part in a free consultation with Attorney Paul Arnold at a local pro bono clinic and the consultation was fully concluded at that time. The caller continued to call and request services, but we declined services and could not continue returning calls to the caller. We take great pride in the outstanding customer service record that we have earned from our clients--and part of that means prioritizing our time for those clients that elect retain our services.


With that said, Attorney Paul Arnold is very proud of his commitment to pro bono work. Attorney Arnold participates in three (3) separate pro bono service clinics each month: the First Friday Pro Bono Probate clinic at the Leon County Courthouse; the First Wednesday Pro Bono probate clinic at the Gadsden County Courthouse; and one clinic each month with the Tallahassee Bar Foundation at various locations.

It is unfortunate that this pro bono service creates issues like the present review, but that will not impede our commitment to pro bono work throughout north Florida.

reviews.text: Very caring and thorough! Stood by my family and myself until we saw positive results...Very happy with outcome.

In dealing with a relatively small but somewhat complicated estate of a relative, Paul Arnold met with us and provided some excellent sage advice with various options. However, his final recommendation was to take a course of action that may not cost us anything. After spending over an hour with us, he refused to accept any payment. If I have need for a probate attorney in the future, I will certainly be returning to Paul.

If I could give Paul 7000 stars, I would give him 8001. I called attorneys all around Tallahassee and all of them said they couldn’t help me or would refer me to other attorneys that ended up referring me back to the one prior. It was an absolute cluster. Paul was the ONLY attorney that took the time to listen to my situation and legitimately help me. He was attentive, thorough, and made me extremely comfortable with the situation at hand. If I felt anxious over anything, he was quick to calm me down and he kept everything transparent. If I had questions of any sort, he was ready and willing to answer them. I never felt like a bother to him or judged in any way, shape, or form. I would recommend him to anyone that needs probate help, power of attorney help, last will and testament help, or any sticky situation when it comes to estate work. I am forever grateful for his extensive knowledge and I will literally tell everyone about him. You will not regret using him for preparing for sensitive moments like this. I definitely will keep him on when it comes to my family and I will recommend him to any family or friends or any stranger on the road. I truly cannot express it enough for how wonderful he is!

I received a letter from a creditor on a debt that I had sworn had been paid. So I initially thought I could just print out my documents and mosey into court without a care because I knew I was right. However, it became clear to me that it is overwhelming and confusing to deal with big law firms who don't care what you have to say and have bought bundled debts from companies without knowing the history. As soon as I began getting emails from the law firm suing me, I knew I needed help in just communicating. I reached out to our local moms group asking for recommendations and Mr. Churchill was unanimously recommended so I reached out to him.

He was so patient and kind with me and helped me understand what all the legal mumbo jumbo meant and really helped me understand what was going on. He took my case that same day and made time for me, despite the fact that he had to do a lot in a very limited amount of time. The case was dismissed today and I have NO doubt in my mind that had it not been for him and his expertise and knowledge, we would have ended up paying what they were demanding of me. I cannot recommend Mr. Churchill enough. If you are unsure and you are apprehensive about what you are possibly facing with a creditor lawsuit, please give him a call.

I was referred by Witherspoon Law group to Coleman Law group to settle my family estate. I had the pleasure of meeting Jaime Coleman who is a very excellent attorney. I was a bit shocked because I worked for a law firm and many have an intern or a paralegal to call.

Secondly, she is very competent on modern law and governing rules of many jurisdiction and municipalities in Florida. My situation was a complex one as some of the estate had not been probated for four generations; she filed the paper work appropriately and assured that everything was done by the letter of the law. I was impressed with the stellar work her and her staff were able to achieve. All of her staff is totally courteous and professional.

Lastly, I found her pricing to be fair and did not feel overcharged or slighted in the least.

Thanks Jaime!

reviews.text:
We bought a brand new  2020 cyclone rv  July 5, 2019.
We had nothing but problems with it from the very
first day. From holes drilled in the drain pipe for the sink
to lights strobing in the living area and master bedroom,
to awning arms breaking, water leaking  in the door to the
kitchen, back ramp door filling with water,
we even had a situation where the rod for the rear bunks
came out and the right seat crashed down and hit my son
in the back. Bubbling around the roof, lights burned out all over.
it was so bad that every single time that we went to use the rv
I had to go pick it up from the repair center, use it, then return
it to the repair center. It got so bad that I was dealing with heartland
directly. I had my own direct number to a supervisor at heartland.
He would call me or I would call him and we would go over the warranty items
and he would tell me he was approving them line by line as I was on the phone
with him. I would go to pick up my rig and nothing would be repaired!  I would
send an email to him 3-5 days later I would get a response that everything
was approved. 4-6 weeks later I would go to use my rig again and nothing
would be fixed. They would print the work orders and show me in black
and white that everything had been denied. This went on for 1.5 yrs. I got tired of it.
Warranties were expiring or expired and nothing was getting fixed. So in late
January 2021 I made 3 calls to different attorneys. I left messages with
all 3 giving a brief explanation of what was going on and the problems
I was having/dealing with. Tony was the only one who returned my call and within
5 min. I spent over 30 min with him over the phone. He listened to every word that I had to say.
He took notes asked me questions so he fully understood my dilemma and then
Made some suggestions to me of what to do next. The last thing he said to me before
we hung up was “ hang in there and if you ever need anything else call me and ill do
whatever I can to help you”. Well 6 weeks later I called him again and hired him to do a demand
letter. I had hundreds of emails back and forth with heartland. I had videos of the damage, 30-40 repair
orders from 3 different repair facilities, that he had to go through.  Within a week he had prepared a
demand letter, sent it to heartland and received a response from upper management. 1 week and he
was already further up the chain than I got in 1.5 yrs.  He didn’t stop there like a normal attorney would
have considering I only paid him to do a demand letter. He kept on with them back and forth until we
reached an agreement.  We are in the final phases now with heartland and of course we agreed to hire
him on for the negotiations at a separate rate. Tony saved us big time. For what he did for us and is still
currently doing for us was way more and way over the top of what he charged us. There is no way that I
could have accomplished what he’s done. I would recommend his law firm to anyone who needs help
not with just issues like mine but any issues you have. If you want someone who never stops fighting for
you, Tony is the man you want on your side. I won’t divulge our settlement but I will say that we are
extremely pleased with the results, especially considering what it cost us. All this and my wife and have
never physically met Tony. Many many conversations and emails later he got it done!! Thank you again
Tony!
Randy Revis
reviews.ownerResponse:
Randy,

Thank you for that awesome review. Without going into any attorney/client details, our motto is helping client's in need. We are there when you have gone as far as you can go and need a lawyer. You made a leap of faith in hiring our firm, trusting that we could help you out.

I'm so very pleased to read your review. It validates the hard work and dedication our client's get once they hire us to be on their "team".

Thank you again for your kind words! Can't wait to hear all about the RV once it is finished.

Tony Conticello
Conticello, PA

reviews.text: My husband and I had the best experience with Mr. Conticello P.A. reviews.text: I used Conticello, PA to obtain legal advice on how to proceed with a Florida based vacation company.  Due to COVID, we had to cancel our travel plans and as a result, the company issued a travel voucher for the full balance we originally paid.  When we re-booked the trip, they sold us insurance to cover the value of the travel voucher, however, when we had to cancel our plans again, we found out that insurance doesn't cover travel vouchers and the company was also unwilling to give us our voucher back (1 time use only).  Anthony answered all of calls/questions and provided several options for us to proceed.  Within 1 day of sending a demand letter, the company was willing to reissue us our travel voucher.  Anthony also checked in with us to ensure we were able to redeem the voucher without any issues.  Without his help we would have lost a significant amount of money as well as a family trip.  We were extremely happy with his services and would highly recommend him. He is knowledgeable, patient and most importantly, effective!
reviews.ownerResponse:

reviews.text:
Short version: I'd definitely recommend them, but make sure to double read your documents and don't be shy about following up and you will have a perfect experience!

Full review:
We used this lawfirm for our end of life planning (last will, living will, healthcare surrogate, and power of attorney). The firm did a great job when we were getting started: we filled out a questionnaire they sent, scheduled a phone call and got all of our questions answered. It was a 5-star experience! The signing of the papers was also a 5-star experience: we came in, were expected, hosted in a board room. The staff was nice and the whole thing took 30 minutes, even with our getting all of our questions answered.

The reason I went down to 4 stars was the part in between: getting the papers ready. After the phone call/sending back the questionnaire/scheduling the signing, we didn't hear anything for weeks - someone dropped the ball on telling us how to pay (life happens), which halted everything. When the ceremony was coming up, we asked about it and were told that they had drafted documents and we're waiting for our payment (we had not been given payment instructions). Once we made the payment, we got our document drafts but I don't think that the drafts had been ready beforehand: there were lots of typos and my husband's name still had the previous client's name on one of the documents.

We reviewed the documents and brought up these edits in a reply via email. The staff took it very well and made the edits without asking any questions/while being very nice. It made us laugh to read a random name when we looked at the documents but we were glad we didn't sign blindly.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you first and foremost for your business and your time in writing our firm a review. It was a pleasure working with you and your husband.

reviews.text:
The Law Offices of Craig J. Brown are truly exceptional. Thalia Mendoza and Craig Brown make the best team I've ever seen at a law firm. They helped my husband with his car accident case and successfully obtained a settlement for him. Their communication was excellent throughout the entire process.

Amy, the personal injury paralegal who joined the team later, has also been amazing—always in good communication with my husband and making sure everything went smoothly.

I also entrusted this office with my Application for Naturalization (Form N-400), and they provided excellent service in my immigration case. Thalia speaks Spanish, which made a huge difference to me, as it's my first language. It was a great relief to work with someone who understands the language and culture—something that's almost impossible to find at law firms in the Tallahassee and Panhandle area. The office also handles criminal defense, which is their primary focus, and they are adept at navigating the immigration and criminal justice systems. Craig and Thalia are experts in criminal defense and have helped many of my friends and family members who have had legal problems, resolving their cases. They treat you like family, not just clients.

With our case, they called me constantly, thoroughly prepared me for the interview, and took care of every detail. I highly recommend their services for both personal injury and immigration cases!

reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello Joseph,

Thank you so much for your thoughtful review! Even though we took on your case at the last minute, after you had a hung jury with the PD's office, we still were able to obtain a Not Guilty verdict for you at trial. I have to say, it was a challenging case at the beginning, given the little time we had to prepare for trial. There was also a lot at stake, because I always knew you were not guilty, but also because I knew that the state had offered you a 15 year plea offer or that we could lose at trial and you would have received 20+ years in prison even as a first time offender. However, we came through at the end, with your cooperation and that of my office. It brings me immense joy to know that we were able to get you out of that situation, where you were wrongfully accused of a heinous crime. I can't say I was not worried or stressed for you though haha. Glad it all worked out. Feel free to call me should you ever need anything. As always, appreciate you trusting my firm. - Craig J. Our custody battle lasted for almost two years, and, finally, resolved favorably for me and our child avoiding the final trial. I cannot imagine that any attorney in Tallahassee other than Bryan Hesser would stay patiently by my side in a very specific case listening, advising, and advocating calmly but persistently for my child going through four hearings and mediation. First we won a favorable temporary outcome, - the time, which allowed my kid to go through a GI treatment he needed and to grow mentally and get ready for spending more time with his father, as well as for our parental conflict to calm down. Then, during the exchange of offers time, Bryan trusted me to decide on when and what I wanted to do, but at the same time directing wisely and advising constantly. Finally, all work paid off, and we were able to secure a very good timesharing agreement, which needed to be drafted quickly. And this is where I saw Bryan being particularly impressive with his professional skills - I don't imagine I would type as fast as he drafted it perfectly in less than half an hour time.
Overall, the office is very efficient. Bryan is great: professional, and effective family law attorney, likely the best one you can find in this region. I had another attorney prior to him and tried several others - Bryan's intellect, organization, dedication, and ability to grasp on a specific family situation disregarding the labels and standards, is unmatchable. He is also a genuinely nice person, clearly liked, respected, and trusted by our Judge. Big respect and lots of gratitude to Bryan Hesser and Liebenhaut | Hesser Law firm.

reviews.text: I was very impressed with his work and defense.

I’ve only had to have one defense attorney in my life, so I don’t have much to compare to, but Mutaqee Akbar exceeded all of my expectations and then some.

Despite the judge and state doing their best to conspire to stack the deck against him, his knowledge and skills in court allowed him to overcome those odds and prove my innocence on a serious charge. He showed great poise in the courtroom and a thoroughness that proved to be quite the obstacle for the prosecution.

Although court is far more boring than that what we see on TV, Mr. Akbar gave a powerful and eloquent closing argument that truly felt like something pulled from the screen, as well as leaving the prosecutor scrambling to pick up the shattered pieces of her closing argument.

If it were not for Mr. Akbar I have no doubt that I would be in prison for the next 10 years. Although I hope to never again need the services of a criminal defense attorney, I would not hesitate a moment to call Mr. Akbar again. Thank you Mr. Akbar and his team for all of their hard work in keeping me home safe.

reviews.text: After ~2 years, through multiple seasons and case managers, some that have left and didn’t get a chance to meet in person (I have to acknowledge), I have witnessed the firm go through changes and remained resilient professionals. Scott and Wallace kept my beliefs strong and my hard work accounted for.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share this. We truly appreciate your patience and perspective.

Over the years we have represented more than 10,000 clients. During that time we have continued to refine how we serve the people who have trusted us to ensure that we deliver the highest possible service. But our focus has never changed: steady advocacy, personal attention, professional care, maximum results.

As firms grow, team members and roles can change, but our commitment to each client does not. Every case is managed directly by our attorney partners and supported by a coordinated group rather than a single individual, so continuity, progress, and accountability are always maintained.

We are grateful that our team’s professionalism and resilience were clear and that your confidence in Scott & Wallace remains strong.

It means a great deal to know the trust you placed in us was honored. Thank you again for allowing us to serve you.

My father-in-law was injured in an automobile accident involving a logging truck and trailer in December of 2012. My wife retained Mr. Frank due to the obvious negligence of the trucking company at the scene, in hopes of getting help with the medical bills that we were facing. Unfortunately, my father-in-law passed away as a result of those injuries in April 2013. Mr. Frank fought the trucking company along with two forestry companies, who were aggressively defended by several large firms.  The goal was to hold the logging trucks accountable and change their procedures in hopes of preventing another tragedy.  We won this case and settled out of court, due to his experience and diligence. The result did not only bring awareness to the safety issues, but it also provided a monetary settlement over $800,000.00, in addition to covering all medical expenses.  Last but not least, a lifelong family friend.

If you need a personal injury attorney that cares, is compassionate and is willing to go the extra mile to fight for your pain, David Frank is that Attorney! reviews.text:
A lawyer's lawyer.
Attorney Roberts have assisted me on a few occasions and he has always delivered more than I expected.  He has an uncanny ability to get to the root of the issue and apply his extensive legal knowledge to maximize results in your favor. Strongly recommended!

Unfortunately i cannot report such an experience that others here report. I originally saw this office and its google rating and had high hopes to only be taken through a nightmare. I unfortunately was paired up with the "Associate" and not Gary Roberts -- perhaps that's where the issues lie. The attorney i was paired with was Tiffani Brown. After being assigned with this attorney i looked her up and saw that the area of my issue (HOA law) is basically her bread and butter. Having said that, this was far from the case as i had to constantly push her and Rebecca (whom was actually very nice the few times i talked to her on the phone). This issue with started with this attorney around the middle of July and is still not resolved to this day. I constantly had to call the office and ask about statuses and push them to act on things that were seemingly just sitting around. I had time-sensitive situations that Tiffani had no concern for. Another situation where i had requested for documents from HOA where after 10 business days i would be owed a minimum $50\day and she had no idea what i was talking about when i mentioned this, that this was even relevant to the situation, and when mentioned told me that she would essentially have to go look into it if it's even relevant or pertains to the situation -- it's cut and dry, and laid out in F.S 720 that if i request documents (meeting minutes) that i am owed those docs within 10 business days; it was going on 20! She had no urgency to push back on opposing counsel, AT ALL. I had to push her to do everything, feeding her Florida Statutes and Case Law all to no avail. I basically was doing all of the work, having to convince my own lawyer that is being paid by me, and also having to educate her on her own job, duty, and angles to approach. After finally having enough, i told her i would like to separate so that i can find another attorney that is actually going to advocate on my behalf and she sends me the most unprofessional separation\termination letter I've ever seen. it was essentially blaming me for everything and trying to make it seem as though my position is entirely unreasonable. Keep in mind, my position is that i am being denied the right to repair my driveway by my HOA's ARC committee for no reason what so ever but for unreasonable, seemingly retaliatory purposes. The HOA at the time was comprised of 3 people, it's since reduced from that due to this nonsense and the HOA president has gone to neighbor's within the community coercing them into signing paperwork to pass additional ARC requests despite not having actual ARC committee meetings or board quorum. I presume Gary is more professional and capable as i have not had the pleasure to work with him and reviews here seem to give praise, but Tiffani Brown is the one to avoid.
great attorney.

As of June/ July 2020- So far, so good. I was recommended to him by a colleague. Others suggested that I go with a "well-known" law firm since their reputation is better ( tv commercials, reviews, more cases, etc).

However, I will provide an update as my personal injury case (rear-ended by a habitual drunk driver who attempted to flee the scene and was arrested/ bodily injury/ etc.) moves forward as i was advised that the details are in the insurance of both parties.  I'm thankful for my life which is priceless and precious, but I pray that he is able to use his 25+ years of experience to reach the maximum compensation.
***I will provide an update which is why I rated 3 stars at this time.

I hired Ashley at Fournier law based on two things: The need for a business valuation of a small family-owned business. And the need for a correct calculation of marital equity in my nonmarital home. I was told Ashly could handle both. She did not handle either correctly. She also had a hard time with my financial affidavit...she spent (and charged me for*) several hours reclassifying Discover and AMEX accounts to liabilities (despite the fact they were labeled "High Yield SAVINGS Accounts"), and trying to (incorrectly) recalculate things.  The week before mediation, I paid another attorney to thoroughly review my stuff (who verified Ashley's calculation of marital equity was nearly $100K OFF). The other attorney prepared me better for mediation in one weekend than Ashley ever would have. The other lawyer provided me correct calculations and advised me on what to focus on in order to settle in mediation.  Ashley was useless in mediation.  Even before mediation, I had the other lawyer on retainer in case I was unable to settle at mediation. I KNEW I was not moving forward with Ashley - nor will I ever recommend her. (*I was refunded a mere $500 of my attorney's fees at the end of the case when I brought these incompetencies to the Firm's attention).
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Sharon, thank you for taking the time to leave your feedback. I apologize for the delay in responding and for any confusion surrounding your review. I was initially unsure about your comments since Ashley has not been with Fournier Law for nearly two years. To clarify, I reviewed our client management records to see if I could identify your case. I did find one matter that may match. That case concluded with a Marital Settlement Agreement signed on January 31, 2023, and incorporated into a Final Judgment on February 21, 2023. A notice of completed representation was filed after the Final Judgment and after the QDRO was signed by the Court on March 6, 2023. Additionally, I confirmed that a $500 courtesy credit was applied to your account. If this was your case, I regret that your experience did not meet expectations. While Ashley is no longer with Fournier Law and, to my knowledge, is no longer practicing family law, I appreciate you sharing your concerns. Please know that our firm remains committed to providing compassionate and diligent representation to all clients.

I am currently in the process of a very difficult divorce.  My soon to be ex-husband hired a very aggressive and litigious attorney to represent him making the process even more difficult.  I was very fortunate to have been referred to Fournier Law Firm, by a friend.  From the very first meeting at Patty"s office, I was certain that she had the experience and skill that I was in need of.  I decided to hire Patty and her firm to represent me.  One of the best decisions have EVER made!

Patty and Gena are great attorneys.  Whether we are in phone consultation meetings or actual court appearances, they are well prepared and in complete control of the situation.  Their courtroom confidence is an obvious indication of years worth of experience.
In addition, the support staff has been very helpful throughout the process.

There simply aren't words to describe how pleased I am with the services and support provided to me by the Fournier Law Firm.  I would give the highest possible recommendation for Patricia Fournier and Gena Fournier.  I simply cannot imagine going through such and emotional process without them.

These ladies rock! They have worked tirelessly on behalf of myself and my minor child! In all my experiences, I have never come across a more caring, knowledgeable and compassionate legal team!! Patty, Gena and Susan know what they're doing!

Carter is not your average attorney. I have been so impressed with Carter‘s ability to listen, help navigate and advocate on my behalf. He takes the time to make you feel like you are the only client he has and provides the results that anybody would be looking for. Highly recommend him and his team.

Carter Scott from Searcy Denny represented my parents when they were rear-ended, killing my father and leaving my mother in the hospital for nearly a month with a fractured pelvis and multiple broken ribs, as well as significant memory loss from a concussion. Carter treated my mom and I like people, rather than a paycheck, actively listening to us both on how we wanted to proceed, but also advising us on all possible scenarios. Carter and his team (we worked with Angela a lot!) understood my mom’s condition and memory issues at the time and made sure to keep me informed. They were not bothered when I wanted them to call my mom to repeat any information they’d just said to me to make her more comfortable. Angela would walk my mom through the DocuSign process when needed, help her go through all the medical bills she’d receive in the mail, and navigate the various health insurance departments harassing us. The team frequently set up 3-way calls for us when I wasn’t able to be next to my mom.

Carter and his team did everything they could to make us whole, and they treated us like family. I would not hesitate to recommend this firm and Carter’s team to anyone going through something like this in the future. Carter, we can’t thank you enough.

I worked with Searcy Denney Scarola Barnhart & Shipley on a car accident case. The team really helped navigate me through the process, and were very communicative the whole time. With their representation, we were able to reach a settlement. Thank you to Bonnie and Mr. Scott for all their hard work on the case.

I had a malpractice case .A doctor took me off my blood pressure meds knowing I needed them ( pull my medical records and then you'll see all I'm saying is true ) well a few days later I had to be rushed to the hospital. MY BOY FRIEND BEGGED ME TO GO CAUSE HE SAID I WAS WHITE AS A SHEEP LOOKED SICK SO EVENTUALLY WENT JUST AS SOON AS I GOT TO THE EMERGENCY ROOM. I STARTED SHAKING CONTROLLABLE COULD STOP BUT AFTER I WAS GIVING IVs AND MY BLOOD PRESSURE MEDICATION I STOP SHAKING AND STAYED ALL MOST A WEEK IN THE HOSPITAL .MY EMERGENCY ROOM DOCTOR TOLD MY BOYFRIEND IF YOU WOULDN'T BROUGHT HER HERE SHE WAS GONNA DIE TO NIGHT THE EMERGENCY ROOM DOCTOR COULDN'T UNDERSTAND WHY THE PRIMARY DOCTOR TOOK ME OFF MY BLOOD PRESSURE.WELL YOU GUYS DON'T CARE.I DO HAVE A CASE AND I TRIED TELLING THE RECEPTIONIST AND SHE PRETENDED LIKE SHE ASK A PARLEGAL BUT SHE WAS GONE ABOUT 3 SECOND NOW COME ON IT SHOULD OF TAKEN MORE TIME.YOUR RECEPTIONIST WAS TALKING OVER ME ! MY ADVICE TO EVERYONE STAY AWAY FROM THIS LAW FIRM!!! THEY DO NOT CARE. MY TESTIMONY IS TRUE Ty

I will Always recommend Jimmy Gustafson & Carter Scott! Thank you to all at Searcy Denney scarola barnhart & Shipley. You have all be so kind and helpful during a horrific loss in our life & will forever be thankful we choose the right people to put up the fight. THANK YOU 🙏
Forever grateful. This law firm carries the most sweetest &  knowledgeable people ever . They really care for you & will only do what’s best. Thank you all so much ❤️

Until you’ve been through a life changing tragedy one cannot imagine, you need a true professional lawyer and team working tirelessly on your behalf.
